coredata.dc:description: Lysozyme (Lyz) is a naturally occurring enzyme that operates against Gram-positive bacteria and leads to cell death. This antimicrobial enzyme forms the part of the innate defense system of nearly all animals and exists in their somatic discharges such as milk, tears, saliva and urine. Increased Lyz level in serum is an important indication of several severe diseases and so, precise diagnosis of Lyz is an urgent need in biosensing assays. Up to know, various traditional and modern techniques have been introduced for Lyz determination. Although the traditional methods suffer from some significant limitations such as time-consuming, arduous, biochemical screening, bacterial colony isolation, selective enrichment and requiring sophisticated instrumentation or isotope labeling, some new modern approaches like aptamer-based biosensors (aptasensors) and quantum dot (QD) nanomaterials are the main goal in Lyz detection. Electrochemical and optical sensors have been highlighted because of their adaptability and capability to decrease the drawbacks of common methods. Using an aptamer-based biosensor, sensor selectivity is enhanced due to the specific recognition of the analyte. Thereby, in this review article, the recent advances and achievements in electrochemical and optical aptasensing detection of Lyz based on different QD nanomaterials and detection methods have been discussed in detail.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Lysozyme (Lyz) is a naturally occurring enzyme that operates against Gram-positive bacteria and leads to cell death. This antimicrobial enzyme forms the part of the innate defense system of nearly all animals and exists in their somatic discharges such as milk, tears, saliva and urine. Increased Lyz level in serum is an important indication of several severe diseases and so, precise diagnosis of Lyz is an urgent need in biosensing assays. Up to know, various traditional and modern techniques have been introduced for Lyz determination. Although the traditional methods suffer from some significant limitations such as time-consuming, arduous, biochemical screening, bacterial colony isolation, selective enrichment and requiring sophisticated instrumentation or isotope labeling, some new modern approaches like aptamer-based biosensors (aptasensors) and quantum dot (QD) nanomaterials are the main goal in Lyz detection. Electrochemical and optical sensors have been highlighted because of their adaptability and capability to decrease the drawbacks of common methods. Using an aptamer-based biosensor, sensor selectivity is enhanced due to the specific recognition of the analyte. Thereby, in this review article, the recent advances and achievements in electrochemical and optical aptasensing detection of Lyz based on different QD nanomaterials and detection methods have been discussed in detail.

ce:section.ce:para: Lyz is existing in nature as an univalent polypeptidic string that comprises 129 amino acid residues with a molecular weight of 14.3 kDa and an isoelectric point of eleven [1]. It is also an antimicrobial enzyme (1,4-β-N-acetylmuramidase) that operates against bacteria by breaking the glycosidic bonds in the polysaccharidic sidewall of numerous bacteria, leading to cellular membrane damage and eventually, cell destruction and dying [2]. Before exploring penicillin, Alexander Fleming explored the Lyz on the based on of the capability of rhinal discharges to prevent bacterial growth [3]. This naturally occurring enzyme is part of the innate immune and defense system of nearly all mammalians [4] and exists in several biological living things from bacteria and fungi to animal somatic discharges such as milk (300 mg L−1), tears (1267 mg L−1), saliva (2.2 μg mL−1) and urine (1.7–123 μg L−1) [5–8]. The hydrolysis products of Lyz potentially enhance immunoglobulin A (IgA) discharge, macrophage activity and quick elimination of bacterial pathogenic agents [9]. In the laboratory, Lyz is usually considered efficient in opposition to some Gram-positive bacteria, but ineffectual opposed to Gram-negative bacteria [10]. Nevertheless, Lyz, maybe non-directly, can impact Gram-negative bacteria in the living body [11,12]. Enhanced Lyz level in serum is related to several illnesses like leukemia, HIV, meningitis, cancer, and renal diseases [13–15]. Besides, it is considered as a major allergenic protein even in trace amounts in food samples and can cause allergic activity in sensitive individuals [16]. Therefore, variation in Lyz level can be an index to diagnosis and treatment of corresponding diseases and also this highly effective protein is utilized in medical fields as a pattern in pharmaceutical, employing in protein and antibacterial studies and enzymatic catalysis [17,18]. Hence, todays, accurate and sensitive determination of Lyz and introduction the reliable analytical approaches to improve food production and to guarantee consumers protection is a pressing need in biosensing analyses and assays.

Quantum dots (QDs) semiconductor materials are new series of nanocrystals that show quantum size effects in their electronic and optical properties [19–21]. In the past two decades, the physics and chemistry of QDs attracted fundamental theoretical and experimental research attempts [22–24]. These researches have devoted to the development of various preparation methods for the synthesis of versatile QDs with different compositions, shapes and sizes [25–28], the surface modification of QDs with numerous organic molecules or biomaterials [29–32], and also their unique applications in a wide variety of technologies and sciences [33–36]. Particularly, these nanocrystal materials with nanometer-sized diameters have efficient and tunable photoluminescence (PL) properties, with photochemical stability, narrow emission and high brightness, and reveal multiple applications including their use for energy conversion and storage [37–39], light-emitting diodes (LEDs) and display applications [40–42], fabrication of optoelectronic devices [43–45], and especially, sensor applications [46–48]. Attractively, in the case of sensor applications, the QD materials with core-shell and multinary (binary, ternary and quaternary) structures have been also absorbed huge scientist's attentions for the construction of different sensing platforms, exclusively aptasensing platforms [49–51].

Currently, three types of QD nanomaterials are used in the biosensing technology based on carbon, metal and silica nanomaterials [52–55]. The first group that is extensively employed by the researchers for biosensing applications due to its tunable and strong fluorescence (FL) emission properties is the carbon-based QD (C-QD) nanomaterials [52]. They are including carbon dots (CDs) and carbon quantum dots (CQDs), graphene and graphene oxide quantum dots (GQDs, GOQDs), and finally graphitic carbon nitride quantum dots (g-C3N4 QDs). These C-QDs can emit the bright FL and possess the interesting properties of good conductivity, high stability, simple synthetic methods and environmental friendliness. In addition, they have very low toxicity as well as better optical properties compared to other QD nanomaterials [56]. The second group is the metal-based QD (M-QD) nanomaterials that they consisted of the elements of group of IIB (Cd and Zn) and group of VIA (S, Se and Te) of periodic table including CdS, CdSe, CdTe and ZnS (binary), CdSSe, CdSeS, CdSTe, CdTeS, CdSeTe, CdTeSe, CdTeS (ternary) and CuInZnS, ZnAgInS, CuZnInS, AgInZnS (quaternary) structures and also their core-shell structures such as CdS–CdSe, CdTe–CdS, CdSe–ZnS and etc. [57–66]. These M-QD materials show some great advantages like high thermal stability, high stability against chemical degradation and photobleaching, broad excitation spectra, narrow emission spectra, long FL lifetime and strong FL quantum yield [67]. However, some of the most limitations of these nanomaterials are high toxicity and low compatibility [68]. The last group that is lower exploited in biosensing technology compared to two above mentioned groups is the silica-based QD (Si-QD) materials. Si-QDs are nanometer-sized particles of crystalline silicon with the capability to emit the FL blue and red light and they have the potential to use as a FL bio-labeling agent [69,70]. These three significant QD materials can be easily functionalized and modified by different organic ligands, nano or biomaterials (proteins, aptamers, enzymes), quenchers, dyes, etc., and used in different applications such as optical and electrochemical probes in biosensor determination [71].

Biosensors constructed based on aptamers (Apts) are famed as aptasensors. They refer to the new kind of sensor in which the Apt acts as a bioreceptor probe and specifically connects to the targets with high affinity and detects them according to the biological response [72]. These newly emerged aptasensing platforms have been received wide attention due to the huge applications in the determination of unlimited biological compounds [73]. Apts are single stranded DNA or RNA molecules and can be designed and synthesized based on various biomolecule targets through a chemical process called Systematic Evolution of Ligands by EXponential enrichment (SELEX) [74,75]. They can be also developed even against low molecular weight compounds like toxins [76] and can be chemically or physically modified by different chemical tags including nanoparticles (NPs), FL probes, electrochemical indicators, quenchers, or enzymes [77]. In comparison to antibodies, they are better on the basis of size and they are about twenty fold smaller than antibodies [78], as a consequence, they can access the targets easily and perform biological assays more effectively and sensitively [79]. Up to know, various traditional and modern techniques have been introduced for Lyz determination, although the traditional methods suffer from some significant limitations such as time-consuming, arduous, biochemical screening, bacterial colony isolation, selective enrichment and requiring sophisticated instrumentation or isotope labeling, but, present some new modern approaches like the use of aptamer-based biosensors (aptasensors) and QD nanomaterials are a main goal in Lyz detection.

Taking into the account of the importance of the accurate and sensitive detection of Lyz, especially medicinal diagnosis, the scientists suggested diverse detection techniques for Lyz determination based on employing aptasensing platforms. These techniques include the FL [80], phosphorescent (PS) [81], chemiluminescence (CL) [82], colorimetric [83], surface plasmon resonance [84], electrochemiluminescent (ECL) [85], photoelectrochemical (PEC) [86], field effect transistors [87] and quartz crystal microbalance methods [88]. On this basis, the aptasensing platforms are approved to be an efficient and strong method for the determination of biomolecules such as a Lyz. It is worth noting that, in the case of QD nanomaterials, up to now, only the optical and electrochemical aptasensing platforms based on CL, FL, PS, ECL and PEC detection methods have been developed that in this review article, in continuation of our reviews in subjects of aptasensing platforms and QDs [54,89–93], the recent advances in aptasensing detection of Lyz based on different QD nanomaterials and detection methods have been discussed in detail.

Electrochemistry is a convenient strategy for a sensitive and selective detection of a number of compounds in environmental and biological analyses. Todays, to develop the detection selectivity and sensitivity, different advanced materials were exploited to promote and improve the traditional electrodes [94–96]. During the past decades, different QD nanomaterials based on carbon, metal and silica QDs have gained extensive interest in modifying electrode due to their great and unique physical and chemical properties [97,98].

In the next study, an aptasensing platform based on a nanocomposite comprised of MWCNTs, CDs and poly(diallyl dimethyl ammonium chloride) has been introduced for electrochemical determination of Lyz by the previous group [107]. The difference of this study compared to the previous one was not only the use of polymer instead of chitosan but also, they did not rGO. The Lyz-linking Apts were fixed on the nanocomposite through covalent junction between the carboxyl groups of the nanocomposite and the amino groups of the Apts. The modified electrode was specified by EIS, cyclic voltammetry and DPV. By using this nanocomposite, a significant rising of the electrochemical response was observed and improved sensitivity. In this research and in order to study the dependence of the peak current on Lyz concentration, the Fe(CN)6 was utilized as an electrochemical probe. Thereby, by adding the Lyz to the system, it was captured by Lyz Apts on the electrode surface and this process creates an obstacle for electrons and limits electron transportation; thus, giving rise to a reduction in DPV peak current. It is worth mentioning that, this aptasensing platform showed the high selectivity and specificity for detection of Lyz compared to other Lyz analogues like thrombin, BHb, human IgG, and BSA which confirmed the successful design of the method. The observed LOD of this method was lower down to 12.9 fM with dynamic range between 50 fM to 10 nM.

Among the current analytical methods including liquid chromatography–mass spectrometry, gas chromatography–mass spectrometry, optical approaches, chemiluminescent immunoassay, Raman microfluidic sensor and enzyme-linked immunoassay, ECL methods have become one of the predominant analytical techniques owing to their high sensitivity, simple instrumentation, good stability, wide dynamic range and low background [108]. Particularly, the ECL methods based on aptasensing platforms have widely been employed in the biosensing technology [109]. For example, Zhu et al. developed an electroproduced chemiluminescence of luminol at a gold electrode containing Si-QDs for Lyz detection [110]. Commonly, luminol can produce powerful ECL signal in basic conditions but in neutral conditions, its response is very feeble at naked electrodes [111]. More importantly, strong basic media is not appropriate for the construction of biosensor. To dominate this obstacle, the catalyzing efficacies of metal NPs, like that silver, gold, palladium and platinum NPs on ECL reactions have been shown [112–116]. In this study, the outcomes showed that Si-QDs while considerably increase luminol ECL, also operate as energy accepter to build an ECL/RET system with luminol. Consequently, a powerful anodic ECL response was achieved in neutral media at the naked gold electrode, which is appropriate for biosensing usage. Lyz showed obvious preventing impact on the ECL/RET system, on the basis of which an ECL aptasensor was constructed for the sentient detecting of Lyz (Fig. 2 ). The reported LOD of this aptasensor was 5.8 × 10−15 g mL−1 in the dynamic range of 5.0 × 10−14 g mL−1 to 5.0 × 10−9 g mL−1.

Dong and his co-workers developed a method on the basis of encapsulation of black phosphorus QDs (BPQDs) within copolymer nanospheres [117]. In recent years, BPQDs as new type of two dimensional materials have been broadly studied in photocatalysis, lithium-ion batteries, field-eﬀect transistors, and especially sensors [118–120] owing to its quantum confinement and edge eﬀects [121]. Although, theses QD materials are not stable and can to be oxidized simply under the air condition [122]. In this research, in addition to BPQDs, the authors used Ru(bpy)3 2+ to produce a powerful anodic ECL for Lyz detection. Therefore, at first, the unified BPQDs-styrene-acrylamide (StAAm) nanospheres were prepared by encapsulation of BPQDs within St-AAm copolymer nanospheres (BPQDs-St-AAm) and then it was conjugated to the DNA. On the other hand, the Lyz Apts were linked on the surface of gold electrode via the S–Au bonding and eventually, the Apt and DNA were hybridized physically and as a consequence, this hybridization was able to bring the BPQDs-St-AAm on the surface of electrode and could react with Ru(bpy)3 2+ to generate strong ECL signal. In the presence of the Lyz, due to its affinity to Apts, BPQDs-St-AAm nanospheres were released from surface of the electrode, and so, the ECL signal of the system was decreased. The observed LOD of this method was 0.029 pg mL−1 in the range of 0.1–100 pg mL−1.

In another study, an ECL aptasensing platform has been reported by Khonsari and Sun [123]. They used nitrogen-doped GQD-chitosan/AuNPs (NGQD-chitosan/AuNPs) and electron transfer mechanism between NGQDs and Na2S2O8 for Lyz detection. In this research, firstly, they successfully immobilized NGQD-chitosan/Au NPs on the surface of indium tin oxide electrode and then, then the Apts were linked on AuNPs syrface using S–Au bonding. Notably, the insertion of AuNPs increased electrochemical action of aptasensor resulting increment of electron transfers between NGQDs and Na2S2O8 and consequently, enhancement of ECL responses and greater sensibility of aptasensor. Conversely, by introduction the Lyz to the modified electrode, due to its affinity to Apts, it conjugated with Apts and interrupted the electron transfers on electrode surface and thus, a considerable reduction in ECL response was observed. The reported LOD for this aptasensor was 0.8 fM in the dynamic range of 10 fM to 10 nM. The selectivity of the aforementioned aptasensing platform was also investigated by selecting thrombin, Hemoglobin, Immunoglobulin G and HSA as interferants. The results indicated negligible ECL with interfering proteins compared to Lyz.

Photoactive active substances with high photoelectric conversion performance are the main materials to design and construct the highly sensitive and selective PEC sensor [124]. Typically, in PEC biosensors, the photoactive parts convert photoirradiation to electrical signal [125]. So far, many photoactive materials like ZnO, TiO2, MOFs and COFs have been used in PEC diagnosis [126–128]. However, owing to the high charge carrier recombination rate and broad band gap, some of them exhibit some major disadvantages such as low photoelectric conversion efficiency and limited visible-light absorption [129]. QDs with high utilization efficiency of visible light and large absorption coefficient are the best alternative for PEC assays [46]. On this basis, recently, a visible light-activating self-power PEC aptasensor has been introduced by Zhang and colleagues based on VS2 QDs-Bi2S3 heterostructure (Fig. 3 ) [130]. Bismuth sulfide (Bi2S3) with a tight band gap (Eg) of 1.3 eV has the competencies of high absorption index and great usage efficacy of visible radiation [131–133]. It, therefore, has been appeared as a favorable photoelectrode substance lately. However, the gradual rate of charge transportation and rapid recombining of photo-produced electron-hole limit its utilization in PEC detection [134]. Constitution of a heterostructure with a proper semiconducting layer substrate is a favorable route to speed up charge transportation and restrict electron-hole recombining [133,135]. In this research, the VS2 QDs-Bi2S3 heterostructure was achieved through one-step hydrothermal procedure by utilization of VS2 QDs and 2.5-fold photocurrent increase. The prepared QDs have a suitable bio-compatibility. It became clear that great visible-light active substances with amplified photoelectric transformation proficiency are essential to construct powerful PEC sensor [127,136]. The proficient visible light absorption of Bi2S3 nanotube, inherent metallic characteristic of VS2, and well-established band framework between them are important to considerably increased photocurrent. By covalence binding 5′-NH2- ended Lyz Apt on the VS2 QDs-Bi2S3 heterostructure, an autonomous PEC aptasensing platform for Lyz was fabricated. The observed LOD of this method was 1.1 pM in the range of 3.5 pM–70 nM.

Currently, optical aptasensors have been extensively developed owing to their high sensitivity and selectivity, simple operation and quick response. Using Apt sequences as the recognition part and different optical methods as signal transductions have been employed to detect various biomolecules [137]. Generally, there are various optical detection methods including CL FL, PS, colorimetry, and surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS). However, up to now and in the term of optical QD nanomaterials for Lyz detection, only the CL, FL and PS based aptasensing platforms have been presented. So, in the following paragraphs, different aptasensing platforms based on CL, FL and PS detection methods are classified and reviewed.

CL is an optical radiation process in which the light emission occurs during a chemical reaction [138]. In recent years, due to its wide detection range, high sensitivity, easy to automate and convenient operation, CL approaches have been gained increasing researchers’ attention. Although lack of desirable selectivity limits its applications in biosensing technologies, that could be promoted by some unique and versatile materials like Apts composites [139], and antigen-antibody biomaterials [140]. On this basis, very recently, Luo et al. developed the CL method based on GQDs@GO@carbon fiber composites (GODs@GO@CF) and hemin/G-quadruplex structures for selective and sensitive determination of Lyz [141]. Therefore, firstly, the GQDs@GO@CF composites were synthesized and then the Lyz Apt and hemin/G-quadruplex structures were immobilized on the QD composite surface. Hemin/Gquadruplex structures operate as a catalyst of luminal-H2O2 CL reaction and Apt as a recognition element. Similar to previous studies and in the presence of Lyz, owing to the high affinity of target to its Apts, hemin/G-quadruplex structures dissociated from the electrode surface and catalyzed the CL reaction of luminal-H2O2. As a result, the CL of the system was happened (Fig. 4 ). This CL method showed a low LOD of 1.25 × 10−11 g L−1 with a wide linear range of 2.64 × 10−10 to 6.6 × 10−8 g L−1.

During the past years, FL NPs such as semiconductor M-QDs, Si-QDs and C-QDs, have been attracted a huge focus of research and development. Theses FL emitting NPs exhibit excellent optical and chemical properties, such as higher biocompatibility, higher photostability and brighter FL, compared to traditional FL materials and organic dyes. Among them, C-QDs have unique optical properties like excitation dependent multicolor emission and tunable photoluminescence. The FL recovery and quenching of CDs and CQDs can be used for the detection of different molecules [142]. Compared with M-QDs, carbon based QDs showed unique advantages including higher photostability, biocompatibility and water dispersibility, and more importantly, lower toxicity [143]. In this regard, Wu and co-workers described a method on the basis of FL resonance energy transfer (FRET) from CDs to graphene oxide (GO) for FL aptasensing detection of Lyz [144]. It has been found that GO can conjugate with dye-tagged single-stringed DNA (ssDNA) through the π–π stacked interaction [145] and simultaneously quench the photoluminescence characteristic of dyes by FRET. Hence, GO could be a noteworthy FL sensor for protein detecting. In this sensing platform, the CDs-tagged Apt adsorption was performed within the GO layer and the photoluminescence signal of the CDs was efficiently quenched by GO. Adding Lyz could lead to a considerable FRET prohibition and retrieve the photoluminescence response of the CDs owing to the specified combination of Lyz and its Apt and the elimination of the CDs-tagged Apt from GO surface layer. In this sensing method, the targets can be selectively and sensitively detected with a low LOD of 1 × 10−3 μg mL−1.

The next study in this section was about the use of CDs and cobalt oxyhydroxide (CoOOH) nanosheets as an aptasensing platform for synchronous tracing of Lyz and adenosine triphosphate (ATP) by Saberi et al. [146]. CoOOH nanosheets are a type of two dimensional nanostructure which have FL quenching activity and because of the possessing planar structure and large surface area, they are a great candidate to be used in biosensing applications [147]. The origin of prepared aptasensor for the synchronous tracing of Lyz and ATP was founded on combining probes solution contain CDs which were stimulated at 340 nm. When the CoOOH nanosheets were added to the solution, the CDs1-Lyz Apts and CDs2-ATP Apts can be attached on the surface of nanosheets via the van der Waals interactions within the basic plane of nanosheets and nucleobases of Apts. In this situation, the FL intensity of system was suppressed due to the FL quenching effect of CoOOH. In the presence of the targets (Lyz and ATP), Apts twisted around their targets strongly and liberated from CoOOH nanosheets surface and consequently, the suppressed FL intensity of the system was recovered obviously. The plausible mechanism of FL suppressing could be attributed to FRET on the basis of overlaying the FL spectra of CDs and UV–Vis spectrum of CoOOH nanosheets. In this sensing method, the targets can be selectively and sensitively detected with low LODs of 1.8 and 4.0 nM for Lyz and ATP sequentially.

Chen et al. introduced a FL aptasensing platform based on M-based CdTe QDs and inner filter effect (IFE) for determination of Lyz [148]. The IFE of FL indicates the absorption of the exciting and/or radiation of fluorophores by absorbing agents in the detection platform [149]. Relative to the traditional FRET, in the IFE procedure, it is not necessary the intermolecular binding of fluorophores and absorbing agents at a special interval, providing a comparably easy technique for the analytical detecting [150]. As an effective tactic of FL procedure, the IFE has been utilized for several analytic sensing [151–154]. The method proceeds from the assumption that red AuNPs operate as strong absorbing agents of the green FL of CdTe QDs due to spectral convergence. Hence, if the Lyz linking Apt was attached on the surface of the AuNPs, the salt-prompted accumulation of AuNPs (that causes a color switch from red to blue) did not happen and the IFE stayed effective. On the other hand, in the presence of Lyz, it could connect to the Apt and consequently inhibited Apt adsorption on the surface of AuNPs. Accordingly, the salt-prompted accumulation of the AuNPs was happened and therefore, a color change was occurred from red to blue, and green FL appeared due to the IFE suppression. The reported LOD of this aptasensor was 0.55 nM in the dynamic range of 1.0 nM–20 nM.

In the last study in this section, a highly sensitive and selective aptasensing platform has been reported by Qiu and colleagues based on CdTe–CdSe QDs with hemin/G-quadruplex structures using strand hybridization and rolling circle amplification (RCA) for Lyz detection [155]. CdTe–CdSe QDs can act as significant near-infrared FL probes for biological imaging and medical diagnosis because of their little FL context and great permeation of near infrared irradiation [156,157]. RCA, a procedure of one-directional nucleic acid reproduction, can quickly produce several duplicates of DNA/RNA molecules [158]. In this respect, a small priming RNA or DNA can produce a lengthy single-stringed RNA or DNA comprising several hundred of repeated series using a circular DNA framework and specific polymerases [159]. Insertion of tumbling circle reproduction can increase the combined number of QD probes via the strand hybridization process, whereas hemin/G-quadruplex structures are used to easily extinct the FL response of QDs in the proximal parts. The liberated primer after the generation of the Apt-target complexes is utilized as the format for the RCA process by means of polymerase and ligase [160]. Generally, this platform mostly requires a small double-stringed DNA containing a circular template, QD-tagged DNA4, hemin/G-quadruplex- based DNA5, Lyz Apt, and the related enzymes. The detection composed of analyte-prompted liberation of the primer from DNA duplicate, format-activated RCA reaction, product-based string hybridization reaction and FL evaluation. The signal was reinforced by FRET between CdTe–CdSe QDs and hemin/G-quadruplex structures on the RCA products. In the attendance of Lyz, it primarily reacted with the DNA1 to liberate the DNA1 primer from the DNA duplicate, which started the RCA reaction to generate a lengthy-stringed DNA by means of round format and enzymes. Afterward, hybridizing of DNAzyme-DNA5 and QD-DNA4 with RCA product were happened. In this instance, the FL of QDs was suppressed by the vicinity hemin/G-quadruplex structures, thus causing the reducing FL strength (Fig. 5 ). The LOD of this assay was 2.6 nM with a dynamic range between 5.00 and 500 nM.

PS is a kind of photoluminescence related to FL. In contrast to the FL phenomenon, PS substances do not promptly emit light and the re-emitting process takes several hours after the original excitation [161,162]. Compared with FL technique, the long-re-emitted PS could keep away from the scattering interference and self-FL from the real samples and showed better LOD and higher sensitivity and signal-to-noise ratio [163]. On this basis, Mn-doped ZnS QDs and cyclodextrin have been introduced by Zuo and his colleagues for the PS aptasensing detection of Lyz [81]. In this study, they employed the Mn-doped ZnS QDs as the room temperature PS (RTP) correspondent, Lyz Apt as the diagnosis component and cyclodextrin as the modifier. By adding the Lyz Apt to the system, the PS intensity of QDs was quenched owing to the supramolecular indications between the Lyz Apt, cyclodextrin and QDs and the state of the platform was off in this situation. When Lyz was inserted within the platform, due to the higher affinity of the target to its Apt, the Apt dissociated from the surface of cyclodextrin modified QDs, and led to the recovery of PS intensity of QDs and state of the platform was on in this situation (Fig. 6 ). The LOD of this assay was 0.54 nM with a linear range between 5.50 and 44.5 nM. Eventually, this approach was exploited to detect Lyz in urine samples and showed satisfactory results.

These days, the aptasensing platforms are confirmed to be a powerful and efficient method for the accurate, selective and sensitive detection of biomolecules especially Lyz. On the other hand, QD semiconductor materials exhibit excellent electronic and optical properties and are the best selection as analytical probes for biosensing technology. Therefore, in this review article, we tried to summarize and compare the recent advances of electrochemical and optical apatsensing detection of Lyz based on different detection methods including ECL, PEC, CL, FL and PS, and various QD nanomaterials such as metal, carbon and silica semiconductor materials. Despite the production of different types of aptasening sensors for Lys, their use in biologic samples is yet problematic; as a matter of fact, the concentration of the analyzable sample may be slight in actual cases and can be even reduced by considering the diluting steps required to reduce the effect of other biological compounds. Therefore, even if some aptasening sensors can achieve limit of detection values in the range of femtomolar, to reduce positive error probability, selectivity should be as high as possible. The main purpose of designing a sensor is to build a tool that can enter the commercial market, and this is still one of the major problems in the construction and development of Lyz aptasensing sensors. If the problems in the commercialization and industrialization of the sensors discussed in this review are solved, we can hope for a quick diagnosis of Lyz in food, medicine, etc., so that a solution can be found to solve the problems of Lys-sensitive patients. It is hoped that this will be achieved soon.
